964?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] re=
cently raised $15M to revolutionize REST API development.
Even the most gifted craftsman needs the appropriate tools to build somethi=
ng they=E2=80=99re proud of. Unfortunately, the API tooling ecosystem is st=
ill stuck in 2005.
Speakeasy [ https://substack.com/redirect/d8d8bd16-51ab-4dac-9196-17b06920c=
964?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] wa=
nts to make it trivially easy for every developer to build great APIs. To m=
ake that dream a reality, Speakeasy is building a platform to handle the he=
avy lifting of API development and unburden developers to focus on refining=
 the business logic.
From fast-growing innovators like Vercel, Mistral AI, and Kong to hobby pro=
jects by solo devs, engineers use Speakeasy to accelerate their API develop=
ment and adoption.
Hi friends =F0=9F=91=8B,=20
Happy Tuesday!=20
Over the past few weeks, we=E2=80=99ve been cooking something up, and I=E2=
=80=99m excited to share it with you.=20
Let=E2=80=99s get to it.=20
Boring News
Today, we are launching a new daily series, Boring News:
Every weekday, we=E2=80=99ll deliver three important stories from Polymarke=
t. We=E2=80=99ll start with the odds that the market sets and use AI tools =
to try to explain why the odds are what they are.
We=E2=80=99re calling it Boring News because compared to sensationalism, tr=
ibalism, and endless BREAKING chyrons, simply taking the odds that the mark=
et sets and trying to explain why they might be that way is =E2=80=A6 borin=
g.
We=E2=80=99re taking individual humans out of the process as much as possib=
le, and replacing them with two forms of artificial intelligence: markets a=
nd AI.
The video you just watched pulls stories from Polymarket, runs their odds t=
hrough news APIs, Perplexity, and Grok for context, turns that context into=
 scripts with ChatGPT and Claude, and narrates it using my voice from Eleve=
n Labs.=20
Then we share it directly with you via X, YouTube, and podcast feeds. Subsc=
ribe at YouTube [ https://substack.com/redirect/07461b8d-2760-4696-b4bc-cdb=
c335af973?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNp=
U ], Spotify [ https://substack.com/redirect/678c7b50-4fde-4ebe-94b3-0d7a15=
4b4ccf?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]=
, or wherever you listen to podcasts to get it each morning.
To start, we=E2=80=99re selecting the markets and running them through an A=
I-powered pipeline. Over time, we=E2=80=99ll do market selection by algorit=
hm, too =E2=80=93 biggest movers, most liquidity, highest volume, closest o=
dds =E2=80=93 and remove ourselves completely.
Our early version is not perfect. We=E2=80=99re wrestling with the tools to=
 try to get the most accurate and relevant information and the snappiest sc=
ripts without writing it ourselves. It feels a little bit like watching you=
r kid do their homework and restraining yourself from giving them the answe=
r.
But I think it=E2=80=99s both fun and valuable to experiment while the tool=
s are still imperfect so that we=E2=80=99re in the right place as they impr=
ove. It=E2=80=99s a little like the Rox strategy [ https://substack.com/red=
irect/6e1569fd-2775-4815-9652-ccaf2a828848?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7Gn=
A_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] =E2=80=93 use today=E2=80=99s AI to bu=
ild something useful and grow our audience, so that we can improve our proc=
esses and benefit from improvements in the underlying AI models. In short, =
Boring News will only get better from here.
So why are we doing this? And who=E2=80=99s we?
When Polymarket correctly called the 2024 US Presidential Election, I got t=
he strong sense that news had changed forever.
Certainly, it won=E2=80=99t change overnight. It was a =E2=80=9CThe future =
is here, but it=E2=80=99s not evenly distributed=E2=80=9D moment. The combi=
nation of unfiltered information on platforms like X, Substack, and podcast=
s with liquid prediction markets like Polymarket have the potential to deli=
ver something closer to the truth to people.
Markets incentivize correctness, punish bias, and reward truth.
Prediction markets are news with numbers. In the future, news without numbe=
rs will seem archaic, a quaintly imprecise relic from a more biased era.
Personally, for almost a year, I=E2=80=99ve been checking the Polymarket ho=
me page [ https://substack.com/redirect/01a7850e-0161-4317-b77a-eef77661b59=
1?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] to s=
can my news each morning before checking traditional media.
Now, whenever I see a headline, I check the Polymarket odds behind it to un=
derstand how real it is. This is, to be sure, a boring way to get the news,=
 free of the hyperbole, hyperventilation, and hyperpartisanship that infect=
s traditional news media. But it also seems to be more accurate.
That said, most people won=E2=80=99t check the charts every day. We=E2=80=
=99re used to neatly packaged stories that provide the context behind the h=
eadlines. If those packaged stories come with bias and sensationalism, well=
, that=E2=80=99s just the trade we make.
One of the things that technology is best at, however, is eliminating trade=
-offs. As I watched Polymarket during Election Week, and read stories like =
the one about the French Whale [ https://substack.com/redirect/605bbc8a-634=
2-4d0c-9511-37a93dd1cdd0?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKb=
qOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] putting $30 million behind his correct call on Trump=E2=
=80=99s victory, the gears started spinning.
What if you could create a news show that delivers news based on prediction=
 markets? You could pull from Polymarket, run it through LLMs, and deliver =
it practically autonomously. Self-driving news: insert market, get full sto=
ry that changes as the odds do.
Technically, this is something that could not have been built until this ye=
ar.
Prediction markets were too illiquid to be accurate. AI wasn=E2=80=99t powe=
rful or internet-connected enough to provide the relevant context, turn it =
into a script, or deliver the script in audio format with a realistic voice=
 clone. The tools will only get better, and we expect Boring News to contin=
ue to get better as they do (and as we do).
This is also not something that I could have built myself, even with AI=E2=
=80=99s help.
I keep saying =E2=80=9Cwe.=E2=80=9D I have a lot of ideas like this, and I =
execute on basically none of them.
But in late October, 19-year-old Indian cousins Aman Matreja [ https://subs=
tack.com/redirect/7f00c71d-4760-4c1e-acc7-c1e91247900f?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3Qi=
fQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] and Sehaj Pasricha [ https=
://substack.com/redirect/3d64fc5e-45c0-4747-a0ff-77dbf3a5b6c2?j=3DeyJ1IjoiO=
XAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ] cold emailed me abo=
ut a (different) project they wanted to work on under Not Boring. I liked t=
he idea and thought they were excellent, so I asked them to join Not Boring=
=2E
After the election, I told them about the idea for Boring News, and they re=
plied, basically, =E2=80=9CNo problem.=E2=80=9D The next day, they had the =
pipeline set up and the first draft done. With very little direction, just =
some mad ramblings. Which is to say, this is more their doing than mine and=
 I feel very lucky to be working with them.
Around the same time, on November 8th, I got a DM from Mike Wenner. We=E2=
=80=99d worked together when he was at Masterworks and Composer, and he tol=
d me he=E2=80=99d just joined Polymarket [ https://substack.com/redirect/01=
a7850e-0161-4317-b77a-eef77661b591?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaM=
ZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. A few days later, I told him about the idea f=
or Boring News, and he told me they=E2=80=99d sponsor it if we did it.
In under a month, from a half-baked idea, we had a working pipeline, test v=
ideos, and the perfect partner. And now, it=E2=80=99s live.
The more we play with it, the more excited I am about the possibility that =
technology will both make news more accurate and restore trust in the media=
, even if =E2=80=9Cthe media=E2=80=9D looks a little different. Even if =E2=
=80=9Cyou are the media now.=E2=80=9D
One recent example really drove the point home for me.
On November 29th, the BBC published a piece on the Irish elections, Tight t=
hree-way race in Irish general election, RT=C3=89 exit poll suggests [ http=
s://substack.com/redirect/a827ab85-c09f-4a52-9bd0-a9d9d12a4b28?j=3DeyJ1Ijoi=
OXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNpU ]. In an analysis, B=
BC News NI=E2=80=99s political editor, Enda McClafferty, wrote, =E2=80=9CIf=
 the exit poll is right this time, then Sinn F=C3=A9in will be pleased with=
 its performance=E2=80=9D before bravely noting that =E2=80=9Cthe true pict=
ure will only become clear when all results are in.=E2=80=9D
The true picture was already clear on Polymarket, where Sinn F=C3=A9in had =
been languishing below 20% odds for over a month, and where bettors correct=
ly predicted Fianna F=C3=A1il=E2=80=99s victory as early as November 25th. =
Today, Fianna F=C3=A1il is at 99.7%.
Despite my Irish heritage, I don=E2=80=99t normally follow Irish elections =
and I didn=E2=80=99t understand why the market favored Fianna F=C3=A1il whe=
n Sinn F=C3=A9in was ahead in the exit poll. But when Aman and Sehaj ran th=
e odds through our pipeline and asked it to explain why, it explained that =
=E2=80=9Csentiment diverges from the exit poll's first preference results, =
highlighting the complexity of Ireland's electoral system, where vote trans=
fers and strategic voting are crucial=E2=80=9D and =E2=80=9Ccoalition dynam=
ics favor Fianna F=C3=A1il, as both they and Fine Gael have consistently ru=
led out partnering with Sinn F=C3=A9in, making a renewed coalition between =
the two centrist parties the most likely outcome.=E2=80=9D That is what end=
ed up happening [ https://substack.com/redirect/0d7d9001-0416-4119-bc70-0f4=
e183fddf0?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.gb8J5T7GnA_ZlNuaMZjmlXXepKbqOsa8-6m8ExkRNp=
U ].
Starting with the probabilities and asking why they are what they are is a =
valuable flip.
Of course, daily news is just one small piece of journalism. There will alw=
ays be crucial roles for investigative reporting, long-form analysis, and t=
he kind of deep work that helps us understand not just what's happening, bu=
t why it matters. What we're trying to do is make the day-to-day news deliv=
ery more accurate and less sensational, freeing up human resources and atte=
ntion for the kind of journalism that truly requires human insight and expe=
rtise.
To that end, Boring News is still very much a work in progress. We're going=
 to try to put out a short, five minute video every weekday and continue to=
 refine it based on your feedback. You might listen to some stories and thi=
nk, =E2=80=9Cwtf that=E2=80=99s not what happened!=E2=80=9D or =E2=80=9CI d=
on=E2=80=99t agree with that explanation!=E2=80=9D First: blame the AI. The=
n: let us know so we can improve it.
If people like it, we'll work with Polymarket to put more resources behind =
making it as good as it can be.
I believe in David Deutsch's Principle of Optimism: "All evils are caused b=
y insufficient knowledge." And I believe in Joy's Law: "No matter who you a=
re, most of the smartest people work for someone else." Prediction markets =
leverage the latter to solve the former =E2=80=93 they incentivize anyone w=
ith valuable information to contribute it.
That's what makes me optimistic about the future of news. Not because of an=
y one project, but because the tools to find truth are getting better and m=
ore open. Going direct is open. Markets are open. Smart 19-year-olds anywhe=
re in the world creating with powerful tools is open.
We won=E2=80=99t be the only ones who try to build something like this, and=
 that=E2=80=99s awesome.
We're having fun experimenting with these tools to try to make the news a l=
ittle more boring, and hopefully a little more true.
The news is dead. Long live the news.
Thanks to Aman, Sehaj, and Mike for building Boring News with me, and to Cl=
aude for editing!
That=E2=80=99s all for today. Give Boring News a listen and let us know wha=
t you think. We=E2=80=99ll be back in your inbox on Friday.=20
Thanks for reading,
Packy

e=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size:=

=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: =

t: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>But I think it’s both fun and valuabl=

ur audience, so that we can improve our processes and benefit from improvem=
ents in the underlying AI models. In short, Boring News will only get bette=

);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">So why are we doing this? And who&#82=

 “The future is here, but it’s not evenly distributed” mo=

tential to deliver something closer to the truth to people.</p><p style=3D"=
margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px=
;">Markets incentivize correctness, punish bias, and reward truth.</p><p st=
yle=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-siz=
e: 16px;"><strong>Prediction markets are news with numbers. In the future, =
news without numbers will seem archaic, a quaintly imprecise relic from a m=
ore biased era.</strong></p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 5=
5, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>Personally, for almost a y=
ear, I’ve been checking the </span><a href=3D"https://substack.com/re=

ne-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">Now, whenever I see a headline, I check t=

ure, a boring way to get the news, free of the hyperbole, hyperventilation,=
 and hyperpartisanship that infects traditional news media. But it also see=
ms to be more accurate.</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55=
, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">That said, most people won’=
t check the charts every day. We’re used to neatly packaged stories t=
hat provide the context behind the headlines. If those packaged stories com=
e with bias and sensationalism, well, that’s just the trade we make.<=
/p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;=
font-size: 16px;"><span>One of the things that technology is best at, howev=
er, is eliminating trade-offs. As I watched Polymarket during Election Week=
, and read stories like the one about the </span><a href=3D"https://substac=
k.com/redirect/605bbc8a-6342-4d0c-9511-37a93dd1cdd0?j=3DeyJ1IjoiOXAwZ3QifQ.=

4, 55, 55);text-decoration: underline;">French Whale</a><span> putting $30 =
million behind his correct call on Trump’s victory, the gears started=
 spinning.</span></p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);=
line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">What if you could create a news show th=
at delivers news based on prediction markets? You could pull from Polymarke=
t, run it through LLMs, and deliver it practically autonomously. Self-drivi=
ng news: insert market, get full story that changes as the odds do.</p><p s=
tyle=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-si=
ze: 16px;"><strong>Technically, this is something that could not have been =
built until this year.</strong></p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rg=
b(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">Prediction markets were t=
oo illiquid to be accurate. AI wasn’t powerful or internet-connected =
enough to provide the relevant context, turn it into a script, or deliver t=
he script in audio format with a realistic voice clone. The tools will only=
 get better, and we expect Boring News to continue to get better as they do=
 (and as we do).</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);l=
ine-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><strong>This is also not something that =

=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: =

yle=3D"color: rgb(54, 55, 55);text-decoration: underline;">Sehaj Pasricha</=

r: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>After the elec=

the pipeline set up and the first draft done. With very little direction, j=
ust some mad ramblings. Which is to say, this is more their doing than mine=

n: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><s=
pan>Around the same time, on November 8th, I got a DM from Mike Wenner. We&=

an>.</span></p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-h=
eight: 26px;font-size: 16px;">Starting with the probabilities and asking wh=
y they are what they are is a valuable flip.</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20p=
x 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">Of course, d=
aily news is just one small piece of journalism. There will always be cruci=
al roles for investigative reporting, long-form analysis, and the kind of d=
eep work that helps us understand not just what's happening, but why it mat=
ters. What we're trying to do is make the day-to-day news delivery more acc=
urate and less sensational, freeing up human resources and attention for th=
e kind of journalism that truly requires human insight and expertise.</p><p=
 style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-=
size: 16px;">To that end, Boring News is still very much a work in progress=
=2E We're going to try to put out a sho=
rt, five minute video every weekday an=
d continue to refine it based on your feedback. You might listen to some st=
ories and think, “wtf that’s not what happened!” or &#822=
0;I don’t agree with that explanation!” First: blame the AI. Th=
en: let us know so we can improve it.</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;col=
or: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">If people like it, =
we'll work with Polymarket to put more resources behind making it as good a=
s it can be.</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-=
height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">I believe in David Deutsch's Principle of Op=
timism: "All evils are caused by insufficient knowledge." And I believe in =
Joy's Law: "No matter who you are, most of the smartest people work for som=
eone else." Prediction markets leverage the latter to solve the former &#82=
11; they incentivize anyone with valuable information to contribute it.</p>=
<p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;fon=
t-size: 16px;">That's what makes me optimistic about the future of news. No=
t because of any one project, but because the tools to find truth are getti=
ng better and more open. Going direct is open. Markets are open. Smart 19-y=
ear-olds anywhere in the world creating with powerful tools is open.</p><p =
style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-s=
ize: 16px;">We won’t be the only ones who try to build something like=
 this, and that’s awesome.</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: r=
gb(54, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">We're having fun experim=
enting with these tools to try to make the news a little more boring, and h=
opefully a little more true.</p><p style=3D"margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(5=
4, 55, 55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">The news is dead. Long live =
the news.</p><div style=3D"font-size: 16px;line-height: 26px;"><hr style=3D=
